Name: Git - Clone, copy, push from a Package
Description: Clones a repository, copies files from the `Source Package` to the `Destination Path` then commits to the repository.

Parameters.Name: Template.Git.Repo.Url
Parameters.Label: Git Repository URL
Parameters.HelpText: The URL used for the `git clone` operation.

Parameters.Name: Template.Git.User.Name
Parameters.Label: Git Username
Parameters.HelpText: Username of the credentials to use to log into git.

Parameters.Name: Template.Package.Reference
Parameters.Label: Package
Parameters.HelpText: Choose the package to copy files from into the Git repository.

Parameters.Name: Template.Git.Destination.Path
Parameters.Label: Destination Path
Parameters.HelpText: Relative path to the folder to copy items to.  This is the folder name only.

Parameters.Name: Template.VariableReplacement.Paths
Parameters.Label: Structured Configuration Variables
Parameters.HelpText: Target files need to be new line seperated, relative to the package contents. Extended wildcard syntax is supported. E.g., appsettings.json, Config\*.xml, **\specific-folder\*.yaml. Learn more about Structured Configuration Variables and view examples.
